items.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

items.body:
## đźŽŻ Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This is a documentation gap issue for `Azure.Search.Documents` requesting new code examples for `SearchAlias` (index alias lifecycle) and image-based vector search (`VectorizableImageBinaryQuery` / `VectorizableImageUrlQuery`), with sample test files and README updates.

<details>
<summary>đź“‹ Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.Search.Documents`
**Affected API:** `SearchAlias`, `VectorizableImageBinaryQuery`, `VectorizableImageUrlQuery`
**Scenarios:**
- Index alias create/get/delete lifecycle â€” no C# sample or test snippet exists
- Image-based vector search via URL or raw bytes â€” not covered in `Sample07_VectorSearch.md`

**Root ask:** Add `Sample13_IndexAlias.cs` and `Sample14_ImageVectorSearch.cs` test files plus corresponding README Examples section entries to back the prose descriptions added in PR #58180.
</details>

<details>
<summary>đź”Ž Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

The gap was introduced by PR #58180 (commit `1942c3c`), which added links and prose for `SearchAlias` and the image vector query types to the README without creating matching `#region Snippet:` blocks. The `Update-Snippets.ps1` script will fail to populate any README placeholders until backing snippet regions exist.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review PR #58180 to understand what README prose was added and what snippets are expected.
2. Create `sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/tests/Samples/Sample13_IndexAlias.cs` following the pattern of `Sample01_HelloWorld.cs`.
3. Create `sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/tests/Samples/Sample14_ImageVectorSearch.cs` following the pattern of `Sample07_VectorSearch_UsingVectorizableTextQuery.cs`.
4. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 search` to verify README placeholder blocks are populated.
5. Run `dotnet build` and `dotnet test --filter TestCategory!=Live` under `sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/`.
</details>

<details>
<summary>đźŹ·ď¸Ź Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` â€” `Azure.Search.Documents` is a data-plane client SDK library (not a management library)
- **Service:** `Search` â€” explicitly named package and service directory `sdk/search/`
- **Confidence:** High â€” issue body names the package and SDK path unambiguously; reference issues #53344 and #53363 confirm the same "Client" + "Search" label pair for this package
</details>

<details>
<summary>đź‘Ą Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Search` (line 410) â€” only label required is "Search", which is predicted
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** `@efrainretana` `@mattgotteiner`
- **Routing action:** Added "Service Attention" label; no assignment (ServiceOwners path); `@mentioned` both service owners
- **Scan notes:** Scanned bottom-to-top from end of file; no multi-label entries for `%Search` were encountered; the single-label `%Search` entry at line 410 was the first match
</details>
